items.title: Gold, silver prices today: Check retail rates of 24K, 22K gold, 999 silver on 18 April in Delhi, Mumbai, Pune, others
items.summary: On April 18, gold prices in India remained stable after witnessing some gains a day before. Check retail rates of 24K, 22K gold, 999 silver on 18 April in your city.

items.title: Bitcoin’s fragile recovery sets up a big short-squeeze risk
items.summary: Bitcoin's climb past $75,000 faces skepticism from leveraged traders, evidenced by prolonged negative funding rates on futures. This divergence suggests a potential short squeeze if prices continue to rise, driven by ETF inflows and institutional buying. However, significant resistance and bearish bets on options markets remain.

items.title: Gold demand set to remain resilient ahead of Akshaya Tritiya; Bullion remains preferred safe-haven for wealth creation
items.summary: Gold demand remains strong ahead of Akshaya Tritiya, with retail investors viewing bullion as a key wealth creation tool. Despite high prices, the festival drives demand, supported by gold's safe-haven appeal amidst global uncertainties. Younger buyers are increasingly opting for lightweight jewellery and digital gold, while investment products like coins and bars are also gaining traction.

items.title: Trump labels Reed Hastings a 'SLEAZEBAG', questions why Netflix co-founder stepped down: ‘What was his crime?’
items.summary: Donald Trump called Netflix co-founder Reed Hastings a ‘SLEAZEBAG’ on Truth Social after his board exit, as Netflix shares fell 9 per cent on a weak second-quarter forecast and Wall Street digested the end of the Hastings era.

items.title: Who is Srinivas Narayanan? US techie quits OpenAI, plans to return to India to spend time with ‘ageing parents’
items.summary: Srinivas Narayanan, former CTO of B2B Applications at OpenAI, is leaving the company after three years. He plans to spend time with his aging parents in India before embarking on a new professional journey.

items.title: Global Market: Japan says recent yen moves driven by speculative trade
items.summary: Japan's Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama attributed recent yen movements largely to speculative trading, following news of the Strait of Hormuz reopening. The dollar weakened against the yen as traders shifted from safe-haven assets. Authorities have expressed concerns over a weak yen's impact on import costs and inflation, signaling readiness to intervene against sharp currency declines.

items.title: Investors pile into Hungarian assets in bet on closer EU ties
items.summary: Stocks, bonds and the forint are up on hopes that Péter Magyar’s landslide victory will lead to pro-market reform

items.title: The declining appeal of the Hong Kong expat
items.summary: Demand for mainlanders with Mandarin skills is on the rise

items.title: The Halo trade: a fad or the future?
items.summary: Some commentators believe Heavy Assets, Low Obsolescence companies could be an investment antidote to markets ravaged by AI

items.title: The coming global food crisis
items.summary: Hunger and even famine are foreseeable consequences of the war on Iran. Now the world must act to shield the poorest from effects that will continue long after the fighting stops

items.title: When keeping it in cash is the dangerous option
items.summary: Having a safe store of ready money is wise. But holding a significant proportion of assets in cash is a big long-term risk to savings

items.title: Turkey promotes ‘Middle Corridor’ as Strait of Hormuz alternative
items.summary: Officials plan to reopen frontier with Armenia to unlock a Trump-backed trade route between Europe and Asia

items.title: Pune airport reopens after IAF aircraft 'hard landing' incident; flight operations resume
items.summary: Pune Airport operations resumed Saturday morning after an Indian Air Force aircraft incident late Friday night necessitated a runway closure. The runway was cleared and safety checks completed, with departures restarting at 7:30 am and arrivals at 8:00 am. The disruption followed a fighter jet's undercarriage failure during landing.

items.title: Berkshire CEO Abel sold stocks managed by ex-portfolio manager Combs, WSJ reports
items.summary: Berkshire Hathaway CEO Greg Abel has divested stocks once overseen by Todd Combs. Combs departed Berkshire in December for JPMorgan Chase. This move signals a significant shift in Berkshire's investment portfolio under Abel, who took over as CEO in January. Warren Buffett continues as Chairman. Berkshire is expected to reveal more details on its equity holdings soon.

items.title: 5 world market themes for the week ahead
items.summary: Markets are optimistic as U.S. President Trump signals an end to the Iran war. Talks may resume soon. However, upcoming economic data could show slowing business and rising prices. Investors also await the Federal Reserve nominee's Congressional hearing. Tesla earnings are also a key focus for U.S. corporations this week.

items.title: Q4 Results Today Live: HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, Yes Bank to announce Q4 results, Jio Financial, Mastek, Bajaj Consumer in focus
items.summary: Q4 Results Today, April 18, 2026, Live Updates: Catch live updates

items.title: Wall Street Week Ahead: Surging record-high US stocks to wade deeper into earnings season
items.summary: The U.S. stock market has achieved record highs, fueled by hopes of cooling U.S.-Iran tensions and an anticipated robust first-quarter earnings season. Despite war-related concerns, major indexes like the S&P 500 and Nasdaq have seen a swift rebound, with investors now focusing on corporate profits. However, elevated oil prices and potential inflation remain key concerns.

items.title: ICICI Bank Q4 results 2026 preview: Expect healthy NII but flattish NIM growth; pick up in PL may drive loan growth
items.summary: ICICI Bank Q4 results 2026 preview: ICICI Bank is poised to report stable numbers for Q4FY26. Analysts anticipate healthy loan growth driven by personal loans and mortgages.

items.title: Q4 results 2026: HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, Yes Bank, six others to declare Q4 results today
items.summary: Q4 results 2026: Six other companies are Bharat Coking Coal, Network 18, Mangalam Global Enterprise, Nilachal Refractories, Dolphin Kitchen, and Virgo Global

items.title: Federal judge blocks Nexstar-Tegna TV station merger until antitrust lawsuit is settled
items.summary: Federal judge blocks Nexstar-Tegna TV station merger until antitrust lawsuit is settled

items.title: Cement demand to be healthy in Q1FY27E; Likely to hit profitability despite price hikes: Nuvama
items.summary: Indian cement demand is set for a strong start in the first quarter of FY27. Government infrastructure spending is driving growth. However, rising fuel and packaging costs are a concern. Cement companies are increasing prices to offset these expenses. The sector's performance will depend on the balance between cement prices and production costs.

items.title: US Stock Market: Wall Street indexes hit record highs as oil falls with Strait of Hormuz declared open
items.summary: Major US stock indices, including the S&P 500 and Nasdaq, closed at record highs Friday, fueled by optimism over Iran's decision to open the Strait of Hormuz and potential peace talks with the US. This development eased inflation concerns as oil prices plummeted, boosting consumer discretionary and industrial sectors while energy stocks slid.

items.title: Social media outlets told to take down fake NSE accounts
items.summary: The Bombay High Court has ordered social media platforms and domain registrars to remove fake accounts and websites impersonating the National Stock Exchange (NSE). The court granted interim relief to NSE, directing platforms like X and Google-owned YouTube to take down infringing content. This action aims to protect unsuspecting investors from fraudulent stock market guidance.

items.title: Bullish momentum continues, but analysts warn of near-term resistance
items.summary: Indian stock markets rallied for a second week, driven by hopes of peace in West Asia and a strengthening rupee. Easing geopolitical tensions and falling oil prices boosted investor confidence, leading to gains across major indices. While the market shows positive momentum, analysts caution about potential resistance and short-term pullbacks.

items.title: US-Iran war: How effective is Donald Trump’s blockade of the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: Iran has reopened the Strait of Hormuz, raising hopes for peace in the US-Iran war. However, President Trump remains silent on the blockade, which impacts China and Russia significantly

items.title: Jio Financial Q4 net profit falls 14% as expenses spike to Rs 692 crore
items.summary: Jio Financial Services saw a nearly 14% drop in March quarter net profit to ₹272 crore, despite a significant rise in total income. However, its lending arm, Jio Credit, reported a three-fold profit surge to ₹70 crore, with assets under management growing 156%. Jio Payments Bank also experienced substantial growth, with income up 11x and deposits reaching ₹544 crore.

items.title: Even a Rs 15,000-crore buyback fails to cheer Wipro investors
items.summary: Wipro's stock fell on Friday after its March-quarter earnings missed expectations, with a ₹15,000 crore buyback failing to boost sentiment. The IT major reported a 2% decline in consolidated net profit, and brokerages expressed caution due to weaker-than-expected performance and revenue headwinds. Analysts noted continued underperformance compared to peers, despite attractive valuations.

items.title: Nifty 'maturity' sets stage for a bull market: ICICI Direct
items.summary: The Nifty has reached a significant point of maturity. This suggests a strong bottom is in place. Analysts believe this sets the stage for the next phase of a bull market. The Nifty target has been revised upwards. Historically, major corrections have bottomed out and led to substantial rallies.

items.title: TCS shares update on Nashik office case: Says Nida Khan 'not an HR', forms oversight panel, calls in Deloitte
items.summary: TCS has clarified that Nida Khan is not an HR manager, constituted a Keki Mistry-led oversight panel and engaged Deloitte and Trilegal for its internal probe into the Nashik unit case, even as police search for an absconding accused in Thane.

items.title: LNG ships move toward Hormuz after Iran says Strait is open
items.summary: Liquefied natural gas tankers are approaching the Strait of Hormuz, potentially resuming vital fuel flows after Iran declared the strait open. This follows a period of effective closure that impacted global LNG supplies and prices. The situation remains uncertain as conflicting statements from Iran and the US persist, with insurance responses also a key factor.

items.title: Irdai keeps FY27 cession rate at 4%, backs GIC Re role
items.summary: Irdai has maintained the mandatory 4% cession rate for FY27, directing general insurers to place this share with GIC Re. This decision aims to solidify GIC Re's position in India's reinsurance market, despite the entry of new domestic players like Jio-Allianz Re and Valueattics Re, who are poised to challenge its dominance.

items.title: The week in charts: IMF growth forecast, inflation climbs, TCS payout, RBI penalties
items.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.

items.title: Sebi clears IPOs of Avaada, Grand Housing & 2 others
items.summary: The Securities and Exchange Board of India has approved IPOs for four companies, including Avaada Electro, Brookfield-backed Avaada Group's solar PV manufacturing arm. Chennai-based Grand Housing will launch an entirely offer-for-sale IPO by its promoter. Sebi provided observations for Avaada Electro's confidential DRHP filed in October 2025.

items.title: HDFC Bank Q4 results preview: Net profit may rise 9%, NII seen up 6% YoY with stable margins; dividend in focus
items.summary: HDFC Bank Q4 results preview: Analysts expect HDFC Bank to report net profit growth in the range of 5-10% YoY, while its NII to grow in the range of 3-6% YoY

items.title: US extends waiver allowing India and other countries to buy Russian oil
items.summary: The Donald Trump administration has issued a short-term waiver allowing countries to continue buying sanctioned Russian oil loaded on ships until May 16, aiming to stabilise global energy prices amid the US-Israeli war on Iran.

items.title: DeepSeek in talks to raise $300 million at $10 billion valuation, Report says
items.summary: DeepSeek is in talks to raise at least $300 million at a $10 billion valuation, according to a report, as the Chinese AI startup that shook global markets faces mounting capital demands for its frontier models.

items.title: Coming soon: Price hike round 3 in four months for durables
items.summary: Consumers face another price hike on electronics like ACs, TVs, and smartphones by end April. This marks the third increase in four months. Rising costs of plastics and memory chips are forcing brands to raise prices. Daily essentials like soaps and detergents are also becoming costlier. This trend impacts consumer demand significantly.

items.title: Adani bags top spot in richest Asians’ club
items.summary: Gautam Adani has officially claimed the title of Asia's wealthiest individual, overtaking Mukesh Ambani with a net worth of $92.6 billion as of April 16. The surge in Adani Group stock prices has fueled this remarkable comeback after earlier hardships. Adani now proudly ranks 19th on the prestigious Bloomberg Billionaire Index, highlighting a significant turnaround in his financial standing.

items.title: India medical tourism slips below pre-Covid peak amid geopolitics, visa delays and rising Asian competition
items.summary: India's medical tourism is facing challenges. Arrivals have dropped significantly below pre-Covid levels. Geopolitical tensions and visa delays are impacting the sector. Competition from other Asian nations is also a factor. Industry leaders suggest focusing on high-tech, reliable healthcare delivery to attract international patients. Improvements in ties with Bangladesh could help reverse the trend.

items.title: Starlink unable to get signal for India liftoff; faces FDI hurdles amid security concerns
items.summary: Starlink's plan for satellite services in India is stalled. The government is reviewing Elon Musk's company's investment proposal carefully. National security concerns are a major hurdle. Geopolitical events have heightened these worries. The company awaits crucial security clearance. Officials will discuss the proposal soon. India prioritizes national security in satellite communications.

items.title: Video shows ships turning away from the Strait of Hormuz as confusion persists over whether sea lane is really open
items.summary: The Strait of Hormuz remained effectively closed despite Iran's declaration Friday that it is open, maritime freight and oil analysts told CNBC.

items.title: Mortgage rates show signs of falling after Iran war peak
items.summary: Major lenders make rate reductions as markets take some heart from a possible truce in the Iran war.

items.title: RBI’s oil forex window set to fuel rupee recovery
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has opened a special foreign exchange window to supply dollars to state-run refiners, easing pressure on the rupee. This move, likely routed through state-run lenders, aims to reduce the significant daily dollar demand from oil companies. Analysts expect this intervention to lead to rupee appreciation.

items.title: Trump says he had 'no idea' Anthropic's Amodei met with White House about Mythos
items.summary: The meeting comes less than two months after President Donald Trump blacklisted Anthropic's Claude.

items.title: 'I'm the lucky one' - more than one in three young men now live with their parents
items.summary: Last year, the highest proportion of men aged 20-34 were still living at home since at least 2007 as the rising cost of living takes hold.

items.title: Iran declares Strait of Hormuz open to shipping but Trump says U.S. blockade still active
items.summary: The Strait of Hormuz has remained almost completely closed as the U.S. and Iran have disputed the terms of their ceasefire agreement.

items.title: Blue Owl co-founders no longer pledging shares for personal loans
items.summary: Doug Ostrover and Marc Lipschultz had committed firm equity worth more than $1.1bn last year as collateral

items.title: Oil prices plunge as Iran says Strait of Hormuz 'open' during ceasefire
items.summary: Brent crude sinks by a tenth after Iran says the key waterway is open for commercial ships for the rest of the ceasefire.

items.title: Oil slumps as US and Iran declare Strait of Hormuz open to shipping
items.summary: Some tankers reverse course after attempting to transit waterway amid confusion over its status

items.title: US Treasuries Rally as Falling Oil Boosts Interest Rate-Cut Bets
items.summary: Treasuries gained to wrap up a choppy week of war-driven swings, as easing tensions in the Middle East drove oil lower and prompted traders to boost wagers that the Federal Reserve will cut interest rates this year.

items.title: US stocks today: S&P 500, Nasdaq end at fresh records as oil prices tumble
items.summary: S&P 500, Nasdaq and Dow hit fresh records as optimism over a possible Iran-US deal and Strait of Hormuz reopening sent oil prices down, easing inflation and boosting equities.

items.title: Canadian dollar hits one-month high as Iran says key waterway open

items.title: Strait of Hormuz crisis spotlights ship 'flagging' rules as India-flagged vessels face higher compliance burden
items.summary: Ship safety in the Strait of Hormuz draws attention to vessel flagging. India is boosting its flagged fleet. The Maritime Amrit Kaal Vision 2047 targets a significant increase in the global share of Indian-flagged ships. This initiative aims to enhance the utilization of these vessels by 2047. Indian flagged ships receive advantages in domestic waters.

items.title: US Section 301 probe: India textile body TEXPROCIL pushes back on USTR excess capacity allegations
items.summary: TEXPROCIL has responded to US investigations on excess manufacturing capacity and forced labor. The Indian textile body rejects these claims, highlighting strong domestic demand. India's cotton textile sector is primarily driven by local consumption, with exports forming a small portion. Any negative US action could harm trade ties.

items.title: ET Graphics: India to become world's third-largest economy by 2031
items.summary: India is projected to become the world’s third-largest economy by 2031, despite slipping to sixth place in 2025 due to a stronger dollar and GDP base year revision. The report highlights India’s continued position among the fastest-growing major economies and outlines its expected rise to fourth place by 2027.

items.title: Visa's likely to appoint Suresh Sethi as India head
items.summary: Visa is reportedly appointing Suresh Sethi, former CEO of Protean eGov Technologies, as its new India country head. Sethi, with extensive experience in banking and tech, succeeds Sandeep Ghosh. This leadership transition occurs as Visa intensifies its focus on digital payments and regional partnerships, aiming for continued growth and innovation in the Indian market.

items.title: Govt revisits plan to raise wage cap for PF coverage
items.summary: The government is considering a significant increase in the provident fund wage ceiling. Current limits may rise to ₹25,000 or ₹30,000 monthly from ₹15,000. This move aims to prevent workers from being excluded from social security due to rising wages. Discussions are underway with stakeholders to finalize the new ceiling. The Employees' State Insurance Corporation ceiling is also being reviewed.

items.title: Ex-CEO, ex-CFO of bankrupt AI company charged with fraud
items.summary: ILEARNINGENGINES-INDICTMENT/:Ex-CEO, ex-CFO of bankrupt AI company charged with fraud

items.title: 15 banks get government nod to import gold & silver for 3 years
items.summary: India has authorized 17 banks to import gold and silver. This authorization is effective for three years from April 1, 2026. The move resolves a customs clearance delay that had stalled shipments. This decision brings relief to importers and ensures smooth trade of precious metals. The new authorization is valid until March 31, 2029.

items.title: Share buybacks not ‘income’, can’t face I-T levy: Delhi High Court
items.summary: The Delhi High Court ruled that company share buybacks below fair market value do not constitute taxable income, distinguishing them from asset acquisition. The court affirmed that such repurchases are capital restructuring, not profit generation, and upheld the Income Tax Appellate Tribunal's decision in a case involving Globe Capital Market.

items.title: Yields fall as hopes of end to Iran war ease inflation fears
items.summary: USA-BONDS/ (UPDATE 1):TREASURIES-Yields fall as hopes of end to Iran war ease inflation fears

items.title: U.S. oil price plunges below $84 as Iran declares Strait of Hormuz open, easing supply fears
items.summary: Oil prices tumbled more than 11% after Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz open amid a ceasefire deal with Lebanon.

items.title: Vedanta Resources seeks $200 million loan top-up after $350 million deal earlier this year
items.summary: Vedanta Resources is looking to boost its existing $350 million loan by at least $200 million. The company is in talks with Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corp. and First Abu Dhabi Bank to join the facility. This move aims to refinance obligations, cover transaction costs, and support general corporate needs, as Vedanta continues its debt reduction efforts.

items.title: Tanker diplomacy: Trump faces tests from Havana to Hormuz
items.summary: From Cuba to the Persian Gulf, Trump is expected to face fresh challenges across a new arc of tanker diplomacy.

items.title: India-China SCO talks focus on trade security, connectivity under first bilateral consultations
items.summary: India and China held their first bilateral consultations on the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation in New Delhi. Both nations agreed to enhance cooperation on security, trade, and regional connectivity. Discussions focused on implementing SCO leaders' decisions and the organization's future direction. This meeting aims to build confidence and stabilize bilateral ties amid global shifts.

items.title: Tinder and Zoom offer 'proof of humanity' eye-scans to combat AI
items.summary: The tech aims to identify people's irises and stop the rise of fake accounts and malicious scams.

items.title: Ethiopia Bondholders Start Process to Sue Government Over Defaulted Debt
items.summary: A group of Ethiopia’s bondholders formally began a process to sue the government over a defaulted $1 billion debt, according to people familiar with the matter.

items.title: Pune airport runway shut after IAF aircraft makes 'hard landing'
items.summary: Pune airport's runway is currently closed following an incident involving an Indian Air Force aircraft. The aircrew is safe and no civilian property was damaged. Efforts are actively underway to clear the runway and restore normal flight operations. Authorities estimate it will take approximately five hours to resume services. Airlines have been notified of the disruption.

items.title: US move on Russian oil waiver adds pressure as Iran war strains energy markets, India engaged in talks: Sources
items.summary: India faces a tougher energy market as the US will not extend waivers for Russian oil imports. This decision impacts countries already struggling with the Hormuz crisis. Russia has assured India of continued energy supplies, including crude oil, LPG, and LNG. India's oil imports from Russia have significantly increased, making it a key market for Moscow.

items.title: Exporters seek removal of cap on interest subvention
items.summary: Indian exporters are calling on the government to lift the cap on interest subvention. They state current support is insufficient to counter rising global trade uncertainties. The Federation of Indian Export Organisations believes the subsidy scheme needs revision. This aims to provide more substantial relief to businesses facing higher borrowing costs and fluctuating export demand.

items.title: US Stocks | Kailera soars over 60% in Nasdaq debut as weight-loss drug race heats up
items.summary: Kailera Therapeutics shares surged 62.5% in their Nasdaq debut after raising $625 million in a U.S. IPO priced at the top end of its range. Strong investor demand reflects growing excitement around weight-loss and GLP-1 drugs, a market expected to expand rapidly as firms challenge pharma giants like Eli Lilly and Novo Nordisk.

items.title: Corporate Affairs ministry proposes easing company incorporation rules
items.summary: In an effort to boost entrepreneurship, the corporate affairs ministry is poised to revamp the company incorporation process. By consolidating numerous forms into a reduced set of e-forms, the ministry aims to slash bureaucratic red tape. Expect a more lenient approach to KYC protocols and documentation for subscribers.

items.title: US Stocks|Drone maker AEVEX valued at $2.6 billion as shares rise in NYSE debut
items.summary: AEVEX made a striking entrance on the New York Stock Exchange, with its shares jumping 15% on debut. This significant increase points to a heightened investor enthusiasm for companies in the defense industry, especially given the current global unrest.

items.title: Europe could run out of jet fuel in 6 weeks, IEA warns
items.summary: The International Energy Agency warned Thursday that Europe is going to run out of jet fuel in as soon as six weeks.
